channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sony Announces Acquisition of Cinemersive Labs
item.content:encoded:
Sony has announced that it will acquire machine learning and computer vision company Cinemersive Labs.

We have heard about a lot of acquisitions recently, with Shift Up announcing yesterday that it is acquiring the studio of Resident Evil creator, Unbound. We have been informed of another deal, and this time, Sony is bringing in a new partner to strengthen SIE’s Visual Computing Group.

Sony Interactive Entertainment Brings in Cinemersive Labs to Its Visual Computing Group

A new blog post on the Sony Interactive Entertainment website announces the acquisition of Cinemersive Labs. Sony reveals that this deal is part of their efforts to “new ways to push the boundaries of visual computing and deliver richer, more immersive gameplay experiences.” Cinermersive Labs is a UK-based company that specializes in machine learning and computer vision.

Sony will make Cinemersive Labs join their Visual Computing Group after the acquisition, with the company then contributing to their” broader efforts in advancing state of the art visual computing within games.” The work would include applying machine learning to enhance gameplay visuals, improve rendering techniques, and unlock new levels of visual fidelity for players. 

In other news, Saudi-based Savvy Games is set to acquire Moonton from ByteDance for $6 billion. Additionally, Saudi Arabia’s Electronic Gaming Development Company has acquired a 5% stake in Capcom. What are your thoughts on Sony Interactive Entertainment acquiring Cinemersive Labs? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Raven Software’s co-founder announcing his retirement after 36 years at the helm. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Does Pragmata Have Early Access? Pragmata Demo Status, Explained
item.content:encoded:
If Capcom’s Pragmata has sucked you into its airtight vacuum of hype and you want to play it now, we’re going to dive into its early access status and whether you can play it any other way, if not, AKA a demo.

Pragmata has gone from being an alluring-looking adventure to one of the most secretly anticipated games of 2026. Whilst Pokémon Pokopia, Resident Evil Requiem, and Saros have been the big hitters in the early parts of 2026, Pragmata has been consistently accumulating interest, and it’s got more eyes on it than Pragmata’s research station has AI robots—a lot!

Can You Play Pragmata Early Access?

Capcom is not offering early access for Pragmata, and there is no special or deluxe edition offering any such perk.

Pragmata has different editions, from a basic version to a more expensive Deluxe Edition upgrade, but none of them come with early access. It means your first access to the full game will be with everyone else in April 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pragmata Release Date & Exact Start Times

This close to launch, with everything set in stone, don’t expect any late early access promotions. We can’t rule out another delay, even if the revised launch date has been moved up. However, if anything does change, Insider Gaming will let you know.

Does Pragmata Have A Demo?

On the other hand, there’s good news on the Pragmata demo front as there is one, and it’s available on all major platforms the game’s releasing on.

There’s no early access to play Pragmata in its entirety before launch, but there is a free demo: The PRAGMATA Sketchbook demo.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pragmata’s New York Looks AI-Generated on Purpose, Devs Reveal

Steam says this about the demo:

“The gameplay demo, Sketchbook, lets you try out this combat system that engages both sides of your brain, while you also get to experience what it’s like to traverse the lunar research station.”

It’s not the most substantial demo in the world, and it really is only a snippet of Pragmata. The consensus is that it takes around 15-20 minutes to complete, which is a good deal for a free demo!

It’s free to download on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2.

Are you tempted to download the Pragmata Sketchbook demo? If you do play it, let us know what you think: Has it sold you on buying the full game if you weren’t sure already? Message the IG community through our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more on Pragmata, check out the game’s director revealing the reason behind frequent delays.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Road To Vostok Early Access Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
The Road to Vostok is a long and arduous one, full of enemy threats and harsh environments, so it’s best if you start your journey sooner rather than later.

Thankfully, the hardcore survival game is entering early access very soon, so you’ll be able to set off across the border between Finland and Russia in the near future.

If your bags are packed and you’re ready for the trip, here’s when Road to Vostok enters early access.

When Is Road To Vostok Entering Early Access? Release Date, Explained

It’s almost time to enter the post-apocalyptic world of Road to Vostok, as the solo-developed title enters early access on April 7, 2026.

It’s a hell of a project for a single person to undertake, but after numerous demos and positive feedback, it’s almost time to get it out into the world for the masses. However, if you were planning to skip early access and wait for the full release, it might not be worth it.

  • More Insider Gaming: Samson Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

The developer expects Road to Vostok to remain in early access for at least two years, with the full possibility of it climbing up to four or more. With the price expected to slowly rise as more content is added, it could be in your best interest to jump in early, so don’t miss the early access release!

Road To Vostok Early Access Release Times

An official release time for the early access of Road to Vostok hasn’t been revealed, but according to SteamDB, it goes live at 8 AM PT on April 7.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every Video Game Releasing In April 2026

Those of you on the West Coast can start making your way across the border bright and early, but for the rest of you across the world, you’ll have to start a bit later. Luckily for you, Insider Gaming has a table showcasing Road to Vostok’s early access release window, adjusted for a whole host of time zones for your convenience—check it out below:

Are you excited for Road to Vostok to enter early access? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming release hubs, check out the following:

  • Invincible VS Open Beta
  • Replaced
  • Pragmata

item.title: Vitality Finally Became Astralis at BLAST Open Rotterdam
item.content:encoded:
Vitality’s dominance in Tier 1 Counter-Strike is well-established, but at BLAST Open Rotterdam, the roster showed that they are now truly manifesting the dominance that Astralis wrought over the CS scene.

Tier 1 collapses under Vitality’s supremacy

No one will remember the Astralis era better than Vitality captain, Dan ‘apEX’ Madesclaire. The Frenchman was in a position much like the rest of the scene, which was largely helpless to prevent Astralis from winning event after event, including three Major trophies in a row.

Roster after roster imploded, faced with the insurmountable wall that Astralis represented. apEX’s G2 roster was no exception.

Vitality has now done that to their opposition. Falcons, Spirit, Furia, and MOUZ have all buckled to varying extents in chasing Vitality, and Vitality are using their capitulation to further advance their superiority even further.

Prior to Vitality’s 2025, there was a prevailing sentiment that Astralis’ era was not replicable. The standard was too high, the talent too spread out, the anti-strating teams studied meant they wouldn’t stay behind for long.

Astralis invented so many aspects of what is now meta Counter-Strike; no team would ever be ahead enough to win as much as that roster.

Obviously, that assumption was incorrect, even by mid-2025, but what seemed implausible even then was the concept that any roster would replicate Astralis’ tactical dominance over the opposition.

Yes, Vitality were ahead. Yes, they had a perfectly assembled roster with the best player, or close to the best player, in every role. But they still had to play their best to win. That has changed this year, and it is core to why Vitality feels more like Astralis in 2026.

Speaking to Insider Gaming prior to BLAST Rotterdam’s first contest, Vitality’s coach Rémy ‘XTQZZZ’ Quoniam stated, “For me, okay, we won two events in a row. I couldn’t say we played completely our A game, but I feel like the other teams are playing their B, C games right now.”

Saving strats for the Major

The message is clear. Vitality are so far ahead that they do not need to produce their best unless prompted. Otherwise, the superiority of their players and existing synergy can allow them to assuredly win map after map after map.

We finally got a glimpse of what Vitality’s A Game may look like against NAVI in the Grand Final of BLAST Open Rotterdam.

Finally, put under some pressure on Map 2 of Anubis, after sliding from an 11-5 lead to an 11-10 scoreline, Vitality pulled off the unexpected to catch NAVI by surprise and steal away the momentum by assembling the triple boost on the A site.

While the strat is by no means unheard of, it is the mark of how far Vitality are ahead that they can save these kinds of strats for pressure moments.

The moment was deeply reminiscent of Astralis themselves, who consistently were able to pull out surprise set-ups when their enemies least expected it.

Arguably, the most memorable example came at the StarLadder Berlin 2019 Major. Secretly preparing a devastating anti-strat of Liquid’s Vertigo in secret, Astralis pulled out the T-spawn to B-stairs run boost to grab quick control of the space.

Again, Astralis did not invent it, but it’s the ability to have these unknown cards in hand that makes them all the more potent when played.

Vitality’s ability to save strats shows that they are now ahead in the way that Astralis were. There is no hope for the opposition because they are trying to catch an illusion: Not chasing what Vitality are, but what they were weeks or months ago, when they did not have these pocket plays in mind.

It is that aspect which finally completes the ghostly visage of Astralis past, which Vitality now embodies in their new era of dominance.

Worst of all for the opposition, it was one set-up on one map. Only the Counter-Strike gods know how deep Vitality’s stratbook goes.

For the first time since that peak Astralis roster, we may be seeing a team unironically saving strats for the Major.

When apEX looks at the scene that he and his team have desolated, he must remember that he was once one of the pack desperately chasing an Astralis roster that was always two steps ahead.

That must make it all the more satisfying to be in the driver’s seat of this roster and have those levers and secret strats to pull out when there is even a hint of a threat to your unquestionable supremacy.

Feature image credit to BLAST/Sophie Barrowclough

Can anyone stop Vitality? Discuss that on our community Discord Server!
For more CS coverage, see our PGL Bucharest 2026 Tournament Guide.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Xbox FanFest Tour is Coming to 8 Cities in 2026
item.content:encoded:
Xbox FanFest is coming to 8 cities in 2026 to celebrate Xbox’s 25th Anniversary.

Earlier this week, it was announced that the Xbox Games Showcase will return in June 2026, followed directly by a Gears of War E-Day Direct. The company also revealed that, as part of the 25th Anniversary, they will bring back Xbox FanFest this year, and now more information has been shared.

Xbox FanFest Tour Will Feature 8 Unique Events Across 8 Cities

A new blog post on the Xbox Wire reveals more details about the Xbox FanFest Tour. In June, the tour will come to Los Angeles, following that, it will arrive in Cologne (Germany), London (UK), Mexico City (Mexico), Seattle (USA), Sydney (Australia), Tokyo (Japan), and Toronto (Canada), with more locations being revealed soon.

The post also confirms that “each FanFest event will be unique to the location in which it’s held.” With the local Xbox community and the ways they engage with gaming being the center focus. More information about what’s included during the FanFest, the dates for each city, booking details, and other information will be revealed on the Xbox FanFest Website.

The last time players were able to attend Xbox FanFest was during Gamescom 2025, where they were greeted by things like Ninja Gaiden 4 battles on the big screen for prizing, a 10-foot Grounded 2 plush ant photo op, Fallout 76 Fishing Zone, finger skate park, exclusive swag bags, and more.

In other news, a tech insider has claimed that the PS6 handheld will have a more powerful GPU than the Xbox Series S. Additionally, Grand Theft Auto 5 and four more games are leaving Game Pass in April 2026. What are your thoughts on the Xbox FanFest Tour coming to 8 cities? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about The Last of Us online being canceled ’24 hours’ before the public reveal. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
